what_it_does: Proposes a 'subtlety' face for the design-system ledger: an optical/material layer that synthesizes findings from 9 source collections into concrete token and grammar augmentations for shadows, gradients, scrims, chrome/frost, textures, warm panel fills, and semantic opacity, plus a set of unresolved questions for the designer.
observations: Re-bases the existing shadow tokens into an ordered elevation ladder (lift_0, lift_1, lift_2, press) while keeping the live names as aliases.
Introduces new token sections: gradient, scrim, opacity_temporality, and a subtlety _principle.
Distinguishes the blueprint ghost (full-field background, top-right asymmetric, very low opacity) from the diagonal hatch band (closing line and stat band, higher opacity).

Proposes conditional rules: scrim.none for curated slide photos vs scrim.flat_dark for web landing heroes; frost alpha range keyed to card content load; page-ground white vs cream possibly riding the warmth dial.
Frames opacity as encoding realized-ness/assertion, making it an alpha-channel sibling to the existing hue-channel ordinal_axis_as_time rule.
Includes derived generator formulas for shadows, node sphere shading, hatch opacity/pitch, frost alpha, and opacity temporality.
Explicitly routes non-optical findings (catalogue modes, photo selection, content budgets, dark-left panel, etc.) out to other faces.
Ends with open questions for Tim about unresolved conflicts and inferences.
standouts: The opacity_temporality rule reframes alpha as realized-ness rather than raw time, attempting to dissolve a reported inversion.
The blueprint-vs-hatch distinction locks a previously conflated texture pair into two separate mechanisms.
The derived shadow ladder turns elevation into a computable function while preserving existing live values.
The conditional scrim family (slide no-veil vs web flat-dark) makes register a first-class selector.
conventions: References existing live tokens and grammar rules rather than restating them.
Uses 'extends-existing' vs 'new' status tags and cites provenance per collection/page.
Flags conflicts explicitly and offers reconciliation strategies instead of silently overriding.
Separates values (tokens.json) from rules (grammar.json) and principles (_principle).
Breaks from static token tables by proposing derived generator formulas.
Treats alpha, hue, and line style as semantic channels rather than purely decorative choices.
concerns: Several key resolutions are framed as inferences or hypotheses that still need designer confirmation.
The node_sphere gradient is flagged as a possible exception to canon-78 ('every perceptible gradient encodes order').
The page-ground white-vs-cream resolution depends on whether the warmth dial actually governs it.
Some synthesized values aggregate sources with noted outliers (e.g., the 0.18 black device-hero shadow).
The frost blur sub-mode conflict remains unresolved.
notes: A dense, evidence-backed proposal for the optical-material layer that mostly extends existing tokens and rules while keeping values, rules, and unresolved decisions cleanly separated.
questions: Does the warmth dial actually govern the page-ground white-vs-cream choice, or is that a separate hardcoded decision?
Is canon-78 absolute, or does node sphere-shading legitimately encode light/dimensionality rather than order?
Where exactly should the routed-out non-optical findings live (catalogue/gallery modes, photo selection, content budgets, dark-left panel)?
Should the hotspot dot be a chrome token or an organism?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add an interpretive, neutral, file-local layer for the subtlety face; the file does exactly that by synthesizing source findings into token/grammar augmentations and open questions.
apparent_themes: Subtlety as a material system: white reads as paper through warm tint, texture, and translucent separation.
Derived, computable design values over static lookup tables.
Semantic channels: hue, alpha, and line style encode order, realization, and assertion.
Conditional rules selected by register (slide vs web) and content load.
Separation by value, tint, or translucency rather than drawn borders.
intention_signals: It is trying to turn a diffuse set of optical-material intuitions (warm paper, soft shadows, frosted cards, blueprint ghosts, semantic opacity) into explicit, generator-usable tokens and rules while preserving live values and deferring unresolved conflicts to the designer.
novelty: The systematic use of derived generator formulas for shadows, sphere shading, and frost opacity keyed to content load, plus the alpha-channel 'realized-ness' rule as a sibling to the existing hue-channel ordinal-axis rule.
contribution: A structured set of proposed token and grammar augmentations and derived formulas for the subtlety face, together with explicit conflict notes and open questions that a fused system can use to decide which optical-material rules to adopt.
summary_for_embedding: A proposed 'subtlety' face file that synthesizes optical/material design findings into token and grammar augmentations for shadows, gradients, scrims, chrome, textures, warm fills, and semantic opacity, with derived rules and open questions.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the brand's optical-material layer as explicit, extensible design-system tokens and rules while reconciling live values with source-collection evidence.
